description: Validates that the list-optimize skill correctly documents its 4 phases, schema additions, idempotency, cost gating, copywriting integration, the v1.1 phase picker, and pre-flight campaign template validation.

evals.description: SKILL.md has valid frontmatter with name and description
assertions: SKILL.md begins with --- frontmatter block
frontmatter contains name: list-optimize
description field mentions 'qualify', 'normalize', and 'personalize'

evals.description: manifest.yaml declares composesWell with full skill chain
assertions: manifest.yaml composesWell includes consulti-scrape
manifest.yaml composesWell includes email-verification
manifest.yaml composesWell includes cold-email-strategy
manifest.yaml composesWell includes cold-email-copywriting
manifest.yaml composesWell includes cold-email-quickstart

evals.description: SKILL.md describes 4 phases in correct order
assertions: Phase 1 is qualify
Phase 2 is normalize
email-verification runs between phase 2 and phase 3
Phase 3 is research (Perplexity)
Phase 4 is personalize

evals.description: Migration adds columns only — never drops or modifies existing schema
assertions: migrate-schema.sh contains only ALTER TABLE leads ADD COLUMN statements
migrate-schema.sh does not contain DROP, ALTER COLUMN, or RENAME
migrate-schema.sh is idempotent (guards each ADD via PRAGMA table_info)
schema.sql leads table includes the 9 new columns for fresh installs

evals.description: Phase 3 (paid Perplexity) always runs cost preflight before any API call
assertions: perplexity-research.sh prints estimated cost before prompting
perplexity-research.sh prompts y/N unless --yes flag passed
perplexity-research.sh aborts on any answer other than y/yes
--estimate-only flag prints cost and exits without calling API

evals.description: Phase 3 runs only on verified+qualified survivors (cost efficiency)
assertions: Phase 3 default WHERE includes qualification_status='qualified' AND pipeline_status='verified'
--include-unverified flag relaxes the verification requirement
Phase 4 only processes leads with personalization_status='researched'

evals.description: Every state change writes a lead_events row for audit
assertions: qualify.sh writes a lead_events row per lead with event_type='status_changed'
perplexity-research.sh writes a lead_events row per lead
personalize.sh writes a lead_events row per lead (success or failure)
lead_events.event_data is JSON containing the phase name and status

evals.description: Disqualified leads are marked + kept, never deleted
assertions: qualify.sh sets do_not_contact=1 for disqualified leads
qualify.sh never issues DELETE FROM leads
Disqualified leads remain in DB with qualification_status='disqualified'

evals.description: Phase 4 enforces hard constraints on generated openers
assertions: personalize.sh rejects lines >25 words
personalize.sh rejects banned phrases (Dear, Hope this finds you well, etc.)
personalize.sh rejects em dashes, en dashes, double hyphens
personalize.sh rejects merge tags inside the line
personalize.sh rejects exclamation marks and questions
personalize.sh requires either uses_topic OR uses_first_name (XOR)

evals.description: Copywriting integration via optional {personalization} token with spintax fallback
assertions: SKILL.md documents the {personalization|fallback} token format
Token uses E1 first sentence ONLY (E2-E4 unchanged)
Spintax fallback fires when personalization_line IS NULL
Existing campaigns without the token still work (backward compatible)

evals.description: Re-running any phase is safe and processes only unprocessed leads
assertions: qualify.sh WHERE clause includes qualification_status IS NULL
normalize-company.sh WHERE clause includes company_name_normalized IS NULL
perplexity-research.sh WHERE clause includes personalization_status IS NULL
Schema migration is idempotent (no errors on re-run)

evals.description: v1 ships with Perplexity as the sole research backend (per user direction)
assertions: perplexity-research.sh calls api.perplexity.ai only (no Apify or website scrape)
SKILL.md mentions Perplexity as the v1 backend
Multi-backend support deferred to follow-up

evals.description: v1.1 Phase 0 picker is documented and offers four selections
assertions: SKILL.md describes a Phase 0: Phase Picker section
Picker offers: Qualify + Personalize, Qualify only, Personalize only, Skip everything
Selection persists in scripts/campaigns/{campaign}/.metadata.json under list_optimize.selection
Re-runs with a saved selection do NOT re-prompt (idempotent)
--reset-picker flag is documented as the way to clear the saved selection
selection 'both' runs phases 1, 2, 3, and 4
selection 'qualify_only' runs phases 1 and 2 and skips 3 and 4 plus the variable validator
selection 'personalize_only' skips phases 1 and 2 and runs 3 and 4
selection 'skipped' exits without running any phase
personalize_only still respects the verification gate (Phase 3 filters pipeline_status='verified')

evals.description: Pre-flight validate-campaign-vars.sh runs before any Phase 3/4 DB writes when personalization is selected
assertions: validate-campaign-vars.sh exists at scripts/list-optimize/validate-campaign-vars.sh
run-pipeline.sh calls validate-campaign-vars.sh before invoking perplexity-research.sh
Validator hard-fails on unknown {var}/{{var}} tokens not in the lead payload allow-list
Validator hard-fails on bare {personalization} or {{personalization}} (no fallback)
Validator runs a sentinel-lead dry-run roundtrip (POST + readback + DELETE) wrapped in a trap so failure paths still delete
Validator is skipped when selection == 'qualify_only'
Sentinel email uses .invalid TLD (RFC 2606) so accidental sends NXDOMAIN immediately

evals.description: cold-email-quickstart Phase 6a is gated with default = run; skipping cascades to 6c
assertions: cold-email-quickstart/SKILL.md documents the Phase 6a Y/n gate (default = Y)
cold-email-quickstart/references/phase-prompts.md contains §P6.optimize_gate copy
Skipping Phase 6a sets list_optimize.selection = 'skipped' in metadata
When Phase 6a is skipped, Phase 6c is also skipped automatically
Skipping does NOT break copywriting — {personalization|fallback} fires the fallback when personalization_line is null

evals.description: personalization-prompt.md cross-references copy-constraints.md for fallback rules
assertions: references/personalization-prompt.md links to cold-email-copywriting/references/copy-constraints.md
Both files agree on: <=25 words, E1 sentence 1 only, fallback required
Cross-reference makes it explicit that updates to one file should be mirrored in the other
